title: Design and implement the core Intermediate Representation (IR)
description: Define the sealed class hierarchy that is the central contract between parsers and codegen. Must cover: ComplexType (→ data class), SimpleType (→ typealias/value class), EnumType (→ enum class), UnionType (→ sealed class), PropertyDefinition (name, typeRef, nullable, default, constraints), Namespace. This IR lives in the :core module and has zero parser/codegen dependencies. All parsers produce IR; codegen consumes IR.

title: Choose and apply Apache 2.0 license
description: Add LICENSE file (Apache 2.0 text), add license header template for source files, configure the Gradle license plugin to enforce headers on all .kt files. Rationale: Apache 2.0 is the standard for Kotlin ecosystem tooling and includes a patent grant.

title: Project scaffold: Gradle multi-module Kotlin project
description: Set up the root Gradle build with all modules declared: core, parser-xsd, parser-jsonschema, codegen-kotlin, cli, gradle-plugin. Version targets: Kotlin 1.9.x, JVM toolchain 21, JVM bytecode target 17, Gradle 8.x. Use Gradle wrapper (gradlew) so no local Gradle install is required. Configure Foojay Toolchains Resolver plugin so Gradle auto-downloads JDK 21 if not present. Set publishing coordinates: group=io.github.schema2class. This is the foundation everything else depends on.

title: Research: domain survey — validate market gap and identify XML-adjacent extension points
description: Survey the competitive landscape to confirm (or correct) the PRD's core claim that no Kotlin-native XSD/JSON Schema → Kotlin codegen library exists. Check: jsonschema2pojo, quicktype, json-kotlin-schema-codegen (pwall), OpenAPI generators, xjc/JAXB ecosystem state. Gather demand signals (SO questions, issue trackers). Also map XML-related extension points: DTD (sample xbel-1.0.dtd already in samples/), RELAX NG, WSDL/SOAP, xmlutil (kotlinx.serialization XML) annotation mode, Jackson XML. Output: findings doc + PRD competitive table update + beads for viable extension points.
close_reason: Survey complete: docs/domain-survey.md. Gap confirmed for XSD (KAXB abandoned, schema-gen dormant); JSON Schema side has one active competitor (pwall567/json-kotlin-schema-codegen) — PRD claim corrected. Extension points filed: xmlutil mode (1k6), WSDL frontend (emb), trang recipe (yx9).

title: IR: add missing PrimitiveType values (BYTES, URI, FLOAT)
description:
Three primitive types are missing from PrimitiveType and are needed for correct parsing of real schemas:

- BYTES: covers xs:base64Binary and xs:hexBinary (KML colorType, binary attachments). Kotlin type: ByteArray.
- URI: covers xs:anyURI and JSON Schema format:uri. Kotlin type: String (safer than java.net.URI for codegen since URI construction can throw).
- FLOAT: covers xs:float (KML outerWidthType, numeric range types). Kotlin type: Float. Note: xs:double → DOUBLE already exists.

Also clarify xs:integer (unbounded) → LONG and xs:int (32-bit) → INT in the XSD parser mapping docs.

title: IR: add contentProperty to ComplexType for xs:simpleContent
description:
xs:simpleContent + xs:extension is used by every UNECE core component type (AmountType, CodeType, IDType, TextType, etc.) and many enterprise schemas. A complexType with simpleContent has a typed text body value PLUS attributes — neither is modelled by the current ComplexType.properties list alone.

Add contentProperty: PropertyDefinition? to TypeDefinition.ComplexType. When non-null, the codegen emits this as the first constructor parameter (conventionally named 'value'). The XSD parser sets it when it encounters xs:simpleContent > xs:extension, using the extension base type as the property type.

Kotlin output example:
  data class AmountType(
    val value: BigDecimal,
    val currencyID: String? = null,
    val currencyCodeListVersionID: String? = null,
  )

Update core IR and all existing tests that construct ComplexType directly.

title: Research: full complexity survey of XSD and JSON Schema features
description:
Before finalizing the parsers, survey the full feature space of both schema formats to identify any IR gaps or parser edge cases not covered by the current samples.

For XSD, investigate:
- xs:group and xs:attributeGroup (named reusable groups)
- xs:any and xs:anyAttribute (wildcard content)
- Mixed content (complexType with mixed='true')
- xs:union of simpleTypes
- xs:list (whitespace-separated list as a single value)
- xs:redefine and xs:override
- Substitution groups (xs:substitutionGroup)
- Abstract types (abstract='true')
- nillable elements (nillable='true' → allows xsi:nil)
- Facets beyond enumeration: minInclusive, maxInclusive, fractionDigits, totalDigits, whiteSpace
- xs:annotation / xs:documentation extraction for doc comments
- xs:key, xs:keyref, xs:unique (identity constraints — likely out of scope but worth noting)

For JSON Schema, investigate:
- allOf (intersection — does this merge properties or create a sealed class?)
- not (negation — almost certainly out of scope but document it)
- if/then/else (conditional schemas)
- unevaluatedProperties / unevaluatedItems (draft 2019-09+)
- contains / prefixItems / minContains / maxContains (array keywords)
- propertyNames (constrains the keys of an object)
- dependentRequired / dependentSchemas
- $anchor and $dynamicRef / $dynamicAnchor (draft 2020-12 recursive references)
- format keyword (date, time, email, uri, uuid — map to PrimitiveType or AliasType?)
- contentEncoding / contentMediaType
- Dialect differences: draft-04, draft-06, draft-07, 2019-09, 2020-12 — which keywords changed?

For each feature, classify:
- Supported in v1 IR as-is
- Needs IR extension
- Supported with codegen-only handling (no IR change needed)
- Out of scope for v1 (document why)

Deliver findings as notes on this issue. Update the IR and PRD if gaps are found.
notes:
## Research Findings: XSD and JSON Schema Complexity Survey

### XSD Features

#### Found in samples — needs action

**xs:simpleContent > xs:extension** (CoreComponentType_2p0.xsd — used by ALL UNECE data types)
AmountType, CodeType, IDType, TextType, etc. are all complexType with simpleContent. Pattern:
  <complexType name="AmountType">
    <simpleContent>
      <extension base="xsd:decimal">
        <attribute name="currencyID" type="xsd:token" use="optional"/>
      </extension>
    </simpleContent>
  </complexType>
This maps to a Kotlin data class where one property is the text/value content and the rest are attributes.
→ IR GAP: ComplexType has no way to model this. Need contentProperty field.

**xs:attribute use="required"/"optional"** (CoreComponentType_2p0.xsd)
Attributes drive nullability the same as elements but are a distinct XML concept.
→ IR GAP: PropertyDefinition has no kind field — cannot distinguish elements from attributes.
This matters for XML serialization annotation generation.

**xs:restriction on numeric bases with minInclusive/maxInclusive** (KML ogckml23.xsd)
angle90Type restricts double to [-90, 90]. minInclusive/maxInclusive map to Constraint.MinValue/MaxValue — already in IR.

**xs:restriction base="hexBinary" with xs:length** (KML colorType = 4 bytes)
→ IR GAP: PrimitiveType.BYTES needed (covers base64Binary, hexBinary → ByteArray)
→ IR GAP: Constraint.ExactLength missing (alongside MinLength/MaxLength)

**xs:restriction base="float"** (KML outerWidthType)
→ IR GAP: PrimitiveType.FLOAT needed — currently only DOUBLE exists.
Also clarify: xs:int → INT, xs:long → LONG, xs:integer (unbounded) → LONG or BigInteger.

**xs:anyURI** (CoreComponentType uri attributes)
→ IR GAP: PrimitiveType.URI missing. Map to String or java.net.URI (String is safer for codegen).

**xs:list itemType** (KML coordinatesType, itemIconStateType)
  <simpleType name="coordinatesType"><list itemType="string"/></simpleType>
  <simpleType name="itemIconStateType"><list itemType="kml:itemIconStateEnumType"/></simpleType>
A simpleType whose value is whitespace-separated tokens of another type.
→ Maps to AliasType with aliasedType = TypeRef.ListOf(T). Parser work only, IR is fine.

**xs:union memberTypes** (KML dateTimeType = dateTime | date | gYearMonth | gYear)
  <simpleType name="dateTimeType"><union memberTypes="dateTime date gYearMonth gYear"/></simpleType>
→ Maps to UnionType with Primitive variants. IR is fine.

**xs:import with relative schemaLocation** (QualifiedDataType_37p0.xsd imports 80+ schemas)
Critical for the UNECE corpus. Each import is a cross-file TypeRef.Named reference.
→ IR is fine (TypeRef.Named.packageName). Parser must resolve schemaLocation to packageName.

**Both xs: and xsd: and no-prefix (default namespace) variants** (all three seen in samples)
→ Parser work only. XSD namespace URI is constant, prefix varies.

**xs:annotation/xs:documentation with namespace-qualified child elements** (UNECE ccts:Name, ccts:Description)
The documentation is structured XML, not plain text. ccts:Name gives the human label.
→ IR documentation field stays String?. Parser should extract text content of ccts:Name or xsd:documentation as the doc string.

#### Not seen in samples but worth noting

**xs:group / xs:attributeGroup**: Named reusable groups. Common in WSDL, Spring XML.
→ Parser expands them inline (like a macro). IR unaffected.

**abstract="true" and substitution groups** (xs:substitutionGroup): KML uses these.
Substitution groups are an XSD extension point — any element in the substitution group can appear where the head element is declared.
→ OUT OF SCOPE v1. Parser emits TypeRef.Named to head element type with a warning.

**mixed="true"**: Text and element children interleaved. Very rare in modern schemas.
→ OUT OF SCOPE v1. Parser treats as ANY.

**xs:key / xs:keyref / xs:unique**: Identity constraints on element values.
→ OUT OF SCOPE v1 (not relevant to Kotlin class structure).

**xs:redefine / xs:override**: Modify imported schemas.
→ OUT OF SCOPE v1.

**defaultOpenContent** (XSD 1.1, KML 2.3 only): Allows extension elements anywhere.
→ OUT OF SCOPE v1.

---

### JSON Schema Features

#### Found in samples — needs action

**const keyword** (sigmacv: schemaVersion const: 2)
A field with a single fixed value. Common for version discriminators.
→ Map to EnumType with one value, OR AliasType with a literal default. Recommend: treat as a non-nullable property with defaultValue set to the const value literal.

**patternProperties** (cloudify.json: all keys → same type)
  "patternProperties": { "": { "": "#/definitions/dataType" } }
An object where all string keys map to values of one type — a dictionary/map.
→ IR GAP: No TypeRef.MapOf. Needed to represent Map<String, T> output.

**anyOf** (cloudify inputProperty "type" field)
Semantically "at least one" (less strict than oneOf). For codegen purposes, treat same as oneOf → UnionType.
→ IR is fine. Parser work only.

**type as array ["string", "null"]** (very common in draft-07 practice, not yet in samples but universal)
The idiomatic draft-07 way to express nullable. Parser maps to nullable=true on the property.
→ IR is fine. Parser work only.

**maxItems / minItems on arrays** (sigmacv openAlexAuthorIds)
→ Already in IR as Constraint.MinItems / MaxItems.

**maxLength / minLength / pattern on strings** (sigmacv id, photo fields)
→ Already in IR as Constraint.MinLength / MaxLength / Pattern.

**Inline object types inside array items** (sigmacv: array of anonymous objects)
→ Parser generates an anonymous ComplexType, adds it to the model, references it by TypeRef.Named.

#### Not seen but important

**allOf with  + additional properties** (inheritance/mixin pattern — very common in OpenAPI-derived schemas)
  "allOf": [{ "": "#/definitions/Base" }, { "properties": {...} }]
→ Parser strategy: merge all allOf schemas into one ComplexType with superType = ref target. Complex but critical.
→ File separate issue for allOf handling.

**format keyword** (extremely common: date-time, date, email, uri, uuid)
  "format": "date-time" → PrimitiveType.DATE_TIME (already in IR)
  "format": "date" → PrimitiveType.DATE
  "format": "uri" → PrimitiveType.URI (needs adding)
  "format": "email" / "hostname" / "uuid" → STRING (treat as constrained string)
→ Parser work. URI needs adding to PrimitiveType.

**$defs** (draft 2019-09+): Same as definitions, just renamed.
→ Parser handles both. IR unaffected.

**if/then/else, not**: Too complex for reliable Kotlin mapping.
→ OUT OF SCOPE v1. Parser skips with warning.

**unevaluatedProperties / unevaluatedItems** (draft 2019-09+): Advanced constraint.
→ OUT OF SCOPE v1.

**$dynamicRef / $dynamicAnchor** (draft 2020-12 recursive refs): Very niche.
→ OUT OF SCOPE v1.

---

### IR Changes Required (file issues)

1. Add contentProperty: PropertyDefinition? to ComplexType for xs:simpleContent
2. Add PropertyKind enum (ELEMENT, ATTRIBUTE, CONTENT) to PropertyDefinition
3. Add PrimitiveType.BYTES (base64Binary/hexBinary → ByteArray)
4. Add PrimitiveType.URI (xs:anyURI, JSON Schema format: uri → String or java.net.URI)
5. Add PrimitiveType.FLOAT (xs:float → Float)
6. Add Constraint.ExactLength(value: Int)
7. Add Constraint.TotalDigits(value: Int) and Constraint.FractionDigits(value: Int)
8. Add TypeRef.MapOf(key: TypeRef, value: TypeRef) for patternProperties

### Parser Complexity Notes

- allOf flattening: merge property lists from all allOf branches; if one branch is a $ref, set superType
- Anonymous type naming: use parent element name + "Type" suffix (or parent field name capitalized)
- UNECE annotation extraction: look for ccts:Name child element of xsd:documentation for the human label
- Numeric codes as enum kotlinNames: use ccts:Name text content, sanitized to SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
close_reason: Survey complete. Filed 6 follow-on issues: 2 P1 IR gaps (simpleContent, missing PrimitiveTypes), 2 P2 IR gaps (PropertyKind, MapOf), 1 P3 IR gap (Constraint types), 1 P2 parser feature (allOf). Full findings recorded in issue notes.

title: Unit test suite: XSD → IR → Kotlin round-trip
description: Write unit tests covering XSD parsing through the full pipeline. Include test cases using real-world public XSD schemas: Maven POM (pom-4.0.0.xsd), XHTML 1.0, Spring beans schema. Verify generated source compiles. These schemas stress-test xs:extension, xs:restriction, namespaces, and recursive references.

title: XSD parser: XSD 1.0 complex/simple types → IR
description: Implement a parser in :parser-xsd that reads an XSD document and produces a SchemaModel IR. Must handle: xs:complexType with xs:sequence and xs:all → ComplexType, xs:simpleType restriction → SimpleType or EnumType, xs:enumeration facets → EnumType values, xs:element minOccurs/maxOccurs → nullable and List<T>, xs:attribute → property, xs:import and xs:include → multi-file resolution. Use JAXB or StAX for XML parsing. Map xs: namespaces to Kotlin package names.

title: Unit test suite: JSON Schema → IR → Kotlin round-trip
description: Write unit tests that cover the full pipeline from JSON Schema input through IR to generated Kotlin source string. Test cases should include: simple object, nested objects, optional fields, enums, arrays, primitive types. Use kotlin-compile-testing to verify generated source actually compiles. These tests are the regression harness for all future parser and codegen work.

title: Kotlin codegen: IR → idiomatic Kotlin source
description: Implement the code generator in :codegen-kotlin that takes a SchemaModel IR and emits .kt source files. Must produce: data classes for ComplexType, enum classes for EnumType, type aliases for SimpleType, nullable types for optional properties, default value expressions, proper package declarations from IR Namespace. Use KotlinPoet for source generation. This is the core output engine.

title: JSON Schema parser: draft-07 → IR
description: Implement a parser in :parser-jsonschema that reads a JSON Schema document (draft-07 baseline) and produces a SchemaModel IR. Must handle: object types → ComplexType, string/number/boolean/integer primitives → SimpleType, enum keyword → EnumType, required array → nullable/non-null mapping, $ref and $defs resolution, array items → List<T>. Use Jackson or kotlinx.serialization for JSON parsing.

title: Add Gradle drift-check task for checked-in generated sources
description: Consumers need CI protection against generated models silently drifting from schemas. Add a Gradle task, e.g. schema2classVerifyGenerated, that regenerates configured schemas into a temporary location and fails if the checked-in generated source directory differs. This should complement schema2classGenerate and be suitable for CI. The task should report clear diffs/paths and avoid mutating checked-in sources.
acceptance_criteria: Gradle plugin exposes a verifyGenerated/drift-check task; task fails when checked-in generated files are stale, missing, or extra; task passes when generated output matches; output identifies changed paths; functional tests cover pass/fail cases without mutating source directories.

title: Complete default and fixed value generation semantics
description: Schema default/fixed values should become real Kotlin constructor defaults when safe, not only nullable = null fallbacks. Audit and complete JSON Schema default handling plus XSD default/fixed handling across elements and attributes. Fixed values should be represented as generated defaults and, where practical, validation that caller-supplied values match the fixed constant. Defaults must use Kotlin-safe literal escaping and should skip or warn on unsupported non-scalar defaults.
acceptance_criteria: JSON Schema defaults, XSD default, and XSD fixed values generate Kotlin-safe default parameters; boolean and numeric defaults are covered; fixed values have tests for generated default and mismatch handling or documented behavior; unsupported defaults warn and do not generate invalid Kotlin.

title: Configurable null omission for optional generated properties
description: Generated serializers should be able to omit absent optional values instead of emitting explicit nulls. Add a per-schema/per-generation option for null omission semantics across annotation modes. Jackson mode should support @JsonInclude(JsonInclude.Include.NON_NULL) or equivalent class/property-level emission. kotlinx.serialization mode should document and support encodeDefaults=false/null omission expectations, and generated defaults should not force unwanted wire nulls. The option should be driven by schema optionality and configurable by CLI/Gradle/library APIs.
acceptance_criteria: Jackson-generated optional properties omit nulls when the option is enabled; kotlinx-generated models round-trip with absent optional fields under the documented encoder configuration; CLI and Gradle expose the setting; tests cover optional nullable fields, required nullable fields if representable, and defaulted optional fields.

title: Configurable friendly names for generated properties and types
description: Generated models need readable Kotlin identifiers even when schemas use abbreviated or cryptic wire names. Implement a format-neutral naming pipeline with separate property-name and type-name concerns. Precedence should support: programmatic NamingStrategy hook, external binding/sidecar file keyed by IR type/property path, schema extensions such as x-object-name and x-object-type, sanitized title/description-derived conventions, then wire-name fallback. Conventions should sanitize punctuation, spaces, hyphens, reserved words, and collisions; for arrays, derive plural property names from item type/title where possible. Avoid requiring Kotlin-specific metadata in shared schemas.
acceptance_criteria: Property and type names can be overridden independently; JSON Schema title is sanitized and used as a convention fallback; array property names can derive from item titles; external bindings work without modifying source schemas; tests cover collisions/reserved words and object-vs-property title ambiguity.

title: XSD parser: xs:group and xs:attributeGroup support
description: Discovered while selecting round-trip fixtures (schema2class-bzb): schemas built on named model groups — XHTML 1.0, Spring beans, many W3C schemas — currently produce empty or partial ComplexTypes because XsdParser only reads xs:sequence/xs:all element children and direct xs:attribute children. Needs: top-level xs:group/xs:attributeGroup registration, ref-resolution at use sites (inline the group's particles/attributes into the referencing type), and recursion guard for self-referencing groups. Once done, add XHTML or Spring beans as a round-trip fixture to XsdRoundTripTest.

title: Mixed-format projects: XSD + JSON Schema inputs in one generation run
description: Owner's actual workflow (clarified July 2026): an .xsd and a schema.json together define valid XML payloads — one XML element's text content is a JSON document conforming to the JSON Schema. This does NOT require IR-level cross-format linkage: the JSON-bearing element stays String in the envelope class, and the payload class is generated from the schema.json independently. What must work: (1) Gradle plugin and CLI accept multiple schema inputs of BOTH formats in one invocation; (2) per-schema package configuration so outputs land in coherent, non-colliding packages; (3) a documented sample showing the pattern (envelope.payloadField = Json.encodeToString(payload)). Optional future enhancement, only if demand materializes: a config hint marking an element as JSON-carrying to generate a typed convenience accessor — do not build speculative IR linkage. Acceptance: a sample .xsd + schema.json pair generates both class sets in one run and they compile together.

title: Annotation mode: xmlutil XML annotations for XSD-sourced models
description: Emit pdvrieze/xmlutil annotations (@XmlSerialName, @XmlElement, @XmlValue, @XmlChildrenName) on generated classes so XSD-sourced models round-trip XML via kotlinx.serialization on Kotlin Multiplatform. xmlutil is the de-facto kotlinx.serialization XML format (docs/domain-survey.md). Mapping: PropertyKind.ELEMENT → @XmlElement(true), ATTRIBUTE → @XmlElement(false), CONTENT (simpleContent contentProperty) → @XmlValue; type/element schema names + target namespace → @XmlSerialName. This is the concrete consumer that justifies schema2class-jo4 (PropertyKind). Builds on the kotlinx.serialization annotation mode.

title: XSD parser: xs:import and xs:include multi-file resolution
description: This was in the original XSD parser scope (schema2class-5q4) but was cut from the initial implementation — XsdParser currently parses a single document and resolves unknown QNames to TypeRef.Named by local name only. Real-world schemas require it: samples/uncefact/data/standard/QualifiedDataType_37p0.xsd has 80+ xs:import statements. Needs: schemaLocation resolution relative to the source file, a parse-once cache keyed by resolved location, xs:include (same targetNamespace, splice) vs xs:import (different targetNamespace, cross-package TypeRef.Named with packageName set). Depends on the namespace-to-package mapping strategy to know which package an imported type lands in.

title: JSON Schema parser: handle allOf for inheritance and property merging
description:
allOf is very common in JSON Schema, especially in OpenAPI-derived schemas. Two main patterns:

1. Inheritance: allOf: [{ $ref: '#/definitions/Base' }, { properties: {...}, required: [...] }]
   → Map to ComplexType with superType = TypeRef.Named('Base') and properties from the inline schema merged in.

2. Pure intersection (multiple type constraints): allOf: [{ minLength: 1 }, { maxLength: 100 }]
   → Flatten all constraints onto the current type/property. No IR change needed.

Pattern 1 is far more common. Implement: if an allOf branch is a pure $ref (no other keywords), treat it as superType. Merge properties from all other branches into the ComplexType's property list. If two branches define the same property name, last wins (document this).

This is a parser-only concern — the IR already supports superType on ComplexType.

title: IR: add TypeRef.MapOf for dictionary/patternProperties types
description:
JSON Schema patternProperties and some XSD patterns produce dictionary-typed values: object where all keys are strings and all values are the same type. Example from cloudify.json: patternProperties: { "": { $ref: "#/definitions/dataType" } }.

Add TypeRef.MapOf(key: TypeRef, value: TypeRef) to the TypeRef sealed class. In practice key will almost always be TypeRef.Primitive(STRING). Codegen emits Map<K, V>.

Alternatively, consider whether additionalProperties: { $ref } in JSON Schema should also produce MapOf. It should — both patterns mean 'string-keyed dictionary of T'.

title: IR: add PropertyKind (ELEMENT/ATTRIBUTE/CONTENT) to PropertyDefinition
description:
XSD distinguishes xs:element children from xs:attribute children. Both map to PropertyDefinition today, but the distinction matters for XML serialization annotation generation (future kotlinx.serialization XML mode, JAXB-style annotations). Without this, the codegen cannot emit correct @XmlAttribute vs @XmlElement annotations.

Add enum PropertyKind { ELEMENT, ATTRIBUTE, CONTENT } to PropertyDefinition with a default of ELEMENT for backward compatibility. CONTENT is used for the contentProperty case (xs:simpleContent value). JSON Schema properties are always ELEMENT kind.

This is a non-breaking IR addition since existing code that doesn't need the distinction can ignore the field.
notes: Priority context from domain survey (docs/domain-survey.md): PropertyKind is the prerequisite for both XML annotation modes — xmlutil (schema2class-1k6, the KMP path) and Jackson XML (schema2class-n0g). Without it, XSD-sourced models cannot round-trip XML.

title: Integration tests: generate → compile → serialize/deserialize
description: Write end-to-end integration tests that go beyond unit tests: generate Kotlin source from a real schema, compile it with the Kotlin compiler, instantiate the generated classes, and round-trip serialize/deserialize real XML/JSON documents through them. Test against: Maven POM XSD + a real pom.xml, JSON Schema for GeoJSON + a GeoJSON document. These tests catch codegen bugs that compile but fail at runtime.

title: CLI: schema2class generate command
description: Implement a CLI in :cli that wraps the library for one-off and scripted use. Commands: 'generate --input <schema-file-or-dir> --output <dir> --format [xsd|jsonschema|auto] --annotations [kotlinx|jackson|none]'. Use Clikt for argument parsing. Distribute as a fat JAR and optionally a native binary via GraalVM native-image.
notes: Parity requirement with the Gradle plugin (see schema2class-sqb): accept multiple --input files of mixed formats (.xsd and .json) in one invocation, each with its own package mapping.

title: Gradle plugin: schema2classGenerate task
description: Implement a Gradle plugin in :gradle-plugin that adds a schema2classGenerate task. Plugin must: discover .xsd and .json schema files from configured source directories, invoke the appropriate parser and codegen, write output to build/generated/schema2class/, wire the output directory into the main sourceSet so generated classes are compiled automatically. Target Gradle 8+, Configuration Cache compatible.
notes: Requirement from schema2class-sqb: a single schema2classGenerate invocation must accept multiple schema files of mixed formats (.xsd and .json), with per-schema packageName config. Format detection by extension/content. This is the delivery vehicle for the owner's XML-envelope-with-JSON-payload workflow.

title: Annotation mode: kotlinx.serialization
description: Add an optional annotation mode to codegen that emits @Serializable on data/sealed/enum classes and @SerialName on properties whose Kotlin name differs from the schema name. This is the most-needed annotation mode for idiomatic Kotlin serialization. Must be a compile-time dependency of the generated code (document in README that consumers need kotlinx-serialization-core).
notes: Integration harness ready: integration-tests module compiles generated code in-test (kotlin-compile-testing) and round-trips JSON documents via Jackson reflection. When this annotation mode lands, extend GenerateCompileRoundTripTest with a kotlinx.serialization round-trip (needs the serialization compiler plugin in the in-test compilation) and XML document tests via xmlutil (schema2class-1k6).

title: XSD: xs:extension and xs:restriction inheritance mapping
description: XSD supports type inheritance via xs:extension (adds properties) and xs:restriction (constrains values). Map xs:extension to Kotlin class hierarchy or property inclusion (Kotlin data classes don't support inheritance well — document the tradeoffs and implement the best available mapping). xs:restriction of simpleType should generate a value class or typealias with validation annotation.

title: JSON Schema: external $ref resolution (relative file paths)
description: Original scope included same-document $ref/$defs and circular refs — those shipped in schema2class-e70 (placeholder registration + resolvedRefs set; resolved refs appear as named types, not inlined). Remaining work: $ref to external files via relative paths (e.g. 'common.json#/definitions/Address'). Currently the parser emits a warning and a TypeRef.Named with null packageName. Needs a document loader abstraction, per-document parse contexts, and cross-document type registration so shared types generate one class.

title: Advanced type mapping: XSD xs:choice → UnionType + JSON discriminator support
description: Original scope was oneOf/anyOf/choice → sealed class. The JSON Schema side shipped: oneOf/anyOf produce UnionType (schema2class-e70) and codegen emits sealed class hierarchies with data class variants (schema2class-hbf). Remaining work: (1) XSD parser maps xs:choice inside a complexType to a UnionType instead of silently dropping it — currently XsdParser only reads xs:sequence and xs:all; (2) JSON Schema discriminator keyword handling so variant selection metadata survives into the IR for serialization annotation modes.

title: XSD: namespace-to-package mapping strategy
description: XSD namespaces are URIs (e.g. http://example.com/schema). Define and implement a configurable strategy for mapping these to Kotlin package names. Must handle: URI → reverse-domain convention, configurable prefix override, collision avoidance when two namespaces map to the same package. Document the default behavior and how to override via config.

title: Carry schema documentation into generated KDoc
description: Generated output should preserve useful schema documentation so replacing hand-written models does not create a documentation cliff. Map JSON Schema description and $comment, plus XSD xs:documentation, into KDoc for generated types, enum members, properties, and content properties where available. Sanitize/escape KDoc text safely.
acceptance_criteria: JSON Schema description/$comment and XSD xs:documentation appear as KDoc on generated types and properties; multiline text and KDoc delimiters are escaped safely; tests cover type, property, enum value, and content-property documentation.

title: Separate wire namespace override from package derivation
description: Some XML schemas lack targetNamespace or use a package namespace that differs from the required wire namespace. Add first-class configuration to set the XML wire namespace independently from namespace-to-package derivation. This should be available in CLI, Gradle DSL, and library APIs, and should affect emitted XML annotations/root element metadata without forcing package names.
acceptance_criteria: XSD inputs without targetNamespace can generate XML annotations with a configured wire namespace; package derivation/override remains independent; Gradle and CLI expose the setting; tests cover no-targetNamespace schemas and imported/multi-schema behavior where applicable.

title: Emit runtime constraint enforcement for generated models
description: Constraints captured in IR should optionally become generated runtime guards, not only metadata. Add an option to emit init { require(...) } checks for scalar range, length, pattern, digit, and collection cardinality constraints where supported. This should work independently of the value-class generation path so users can get pragmatic guards in ordinary data classes.
acceptance_criteria: Generated classes can enforce min/max numeric ranges, string length/pattern constraints, and list cardinality with require blocks; error messages identify the property and constraint; option is exposed in CLI/Gradle/library APIs; tests cover valid and invalid construction for representative JSON Schema and XSD constraints.

title: Support numeric enums with unknown fallback
description: EnumType currently separates Kotlin names from serialized values but needs first-class support for non-string serialized values such as integer wire enums. Add codegen support for numeric enum values in Jackson and kotlinx modes, plus an opt-in synthetic unknown/default member for forward-compatible deserialization of unrecognized values. Jackson should use an idiomatic @JsonValue/@JsonCreator or @JsonEnumDefaultValue strategy; kotlinx should have an equivalent documented/generated strategy where feasible.
acceptance_criteria: Integer-valued enums generate valid Kotlin; serialization emits numeric wire values; deserialization accepts known numeric values; unknown values map to an opt-in UNKNOWN member; tests cover Jackson and kotlinx modes or document unsupported mode limits.

title: Security and quality review pass
description: Run a security and quality review across the repository, including local static inspection and a SonarQube scan against sonarqube.esmcelroy.ca for project schema2class using the local token file without exposing credentials.
notes: Expanded review scope to include detekt static analysis and JaCoCo XML coverage wiring for SonarQube.
close_reason: Completed security and quality review pass: hardened XML parsing, guarded Gradle output cleanup, added detekt and JaCoCo/Sonar wiring, ran full verification and SonarQube quality gate OK.

title: Set up GitHub Actions workflows
description: Add CI workflows for pull-request and main-branch validation once a GitHub remote exists. Should run the SDKMAN-aware Gradle test command, cache Gradle dependencies, and report failures clearly.
close_reason: Added GitHub Actions CI workflow for main pushes, pull requests, and manual dispatch. Workflow sets up Temurin 21, uses Gradle caching, runs test detekt jacocoRootReport, and uploads failure/coverage artifacts. Local Gradle rerun was attempted but blocked by approval usage limit before execution.

title: Rename generated and project package root to ca.esmcelroy
description: Update the classpath/package root from io.github to ca.esmcelroy across project source, generated samples/tests, plugin coordinates if applicable, docs, and package mapping defaults. Requires careful migration because it touches public API/package names and test fixtures.
close_reason: Renamed project package/classpath root and Gradle plugin id to ca.esmcelroy.schema2class; updated docs and tests; verified with ./gradlew test detekt jacocoRootReport.

title: xmlutil mode: @Contextual content properties (BigDecimal/date) not honored as @XmlValue
description: Found while building the xmlutil integration test (schema2class-1k6): a contentProperty typed @Contextual BigDecimal (e.g. UNECE AmountType from xs:simpleContent extension of xsd:decimal) fails to decode — xmlutil 0.86.3 classifies the CONTEXTUAL serial kind as element output, so @XmlValue is ignored and decode throws 'Type TEXT does not match expected type START_ELEMENT'. String content properties (TextType) work. Options: (1) emit @Serializable(with = ...) pointing at concrete serializers instead of @Contextual for content properties — requires shipping a small schema2class-runtime artifact with BigDecimal/LocalDate/OffsetDateTime/Duration string serializers (that artifact would also simplify the JSON story); (2) document an XML policy workaround; (3) upstream issue/fix in xmlutil. Option 1 is the most robust and is probably worth doing for ALL @Contextual sites, not just content properties. Repro: GenerateCompileRoundTripTest with AmountType instead of TextType.
close_reason: XMLUTIL mode now emits generated string serializers for BigDecimal/java.time primitives instead of @Contextual so @XmlValue content decodes as text.

title: Parsers: emit Kotlin-safe literals for schema default values (dollar-sign escaping, non-scalar defaults)
description: PropertyDefinition.defaultValue is consumed by codegen as a raw Kotlin expression (CodeBlock.of), so parsers must emit valid, safe Kotlin literals. Two gaps in JsonSchemaParser (default handling is propNode.get("default")?.toString()): (1) a string default containing a dollar sign — e.g. "default": "${{ github.token }}" in GitHub Action schemas — passes through unescaped and becomes a Kotlin string-template injection in the generated source (repro sketch in scratchpad/dollar-default.json shape); fix by escaping $ and backslashes when building the quoted literal. (2) array/object defaults serialize as raw JSON ([] or {...}) which is not valid Kotlin; either map to emptyList()/skip with a warning. The XSD parser must apply the same literal rules when schema2class-81l (default/fixed capture) is implemented. JsonSchemaRoundTripTest.assertSourcesWellFormed already rejects ${{ injections, so a fixture with such a default will lock in the fix.
close_reason: Fixed JSON Schema default literal emission: scalar defaults become Kotlin-safe expressions and non-scalar defaults are skipped with a warning.

title: WSDL frontend: extract xs:schema from wsdl:types and feed XSD parser
description: SOAP remains common in enterprise integration and the current Kotlin answer is JAXWS wsimport → Java (see docs/domain-survey.md). A WSDL document embeds XSD inside <wsdl:types>; a thin frontend extracts those schema elements (plus their imports) and feeds the existing XsdParser, producing typed payload data classes. Explicitly out of scope: service stubs, bindings, operations — this generates payload types only. Depends on xs:import/xs:include multi-file resolution since real WSDLs import external XSDs.
close_reason: Added a WSDL frontend that extracts wsdl:types xs:schema elements, delegates to XsdParser, and exposed .wsdl generation through CLI/Gradle.

title: JSON Schema parser: map format keyword to richer primitives
description: JsonSchemaParser currently treats the format keyword as plain STRING (marked TODO in the parser). Now that PrimitiveType has URI, and DATE/DATE_TIME already exist, map: format:uri/uri-reference → URI, format:date → DATE, format:date-time → DATE_TIME, format:duration → DURATION. Unknown formats stay STRING. Keep this behind a parser option if strictness matters — format is annotation-only per the JSON Schema spec, so some schemas use values that do not actually conform.
close_reason: JSON Schema string format now maps uri/date/date-time/duration to richer PrimitiveTypes, with generated serializers covering java.time integration.

title: XSD parser: capture default and fixed attribute values
description: XSD allows default="..." and fixed="..." on xs:element and xs:attribute, but XsdParser.buildElementProperty/buildAttributeProperty always set defaultValue = null. The IR and codegen already support defaults (JSON Schema default flows through to constructor default values), so this is parser-only work. Map default → PropertyDefinition.defaultValue with proper Kotlin literal quoting for the property type (strings need quotes, numbers/booleans do not). Decide whether fixed also emits a default or a constant.
close_reason: XSD elements and attributes now map default/fixed lexical values to Kotlin-safe default expressions.

title: XSD parser: xs:list and xs:union simpleType support
description: XsdParser handles xs:simpleType only via xs:restriction; a simpleType whose child is xs:list or xs:union currently degrades to AliasType of STRING. Seen in samples/kml/2.3.0/ogckml23.xsd. Mapping: xs:list itemType=T → AliasType of ListOf(T) (whitespace-separated list on the wire); xs:union memberTypes → UnionType when members are distinguishable, else AliasType of the widest common primitive (typically STRING).
close_reason: XSD simpleType now supports xs:list aliases and xs:union as UnionType when members are distinguishable, degrading ambiguous unions to String aliases.

title: IR: add missing Constraint types (ExactLength, TotalDigits, FractionDigits)
description:
Three constraint facets from XSD are not yet in the Constraint sealed class:

- ExactLength(value: Int): xs:length facet (e.g. KML colorType = exactly 4 bytes). Distinct from MinLength/MaxLength.
- TotalDigits(value: Int): xs:totalDigits facet for decimal precision.
- FractionDigits(value: Int): xs:fractionDigits facet for decimal scale.

These are low priority for codegen (they don't affect the generated type) but are needed for complete constraint round-tripping and future validation annotation support.
close_reason: Added ExactLength, TotalDigits, and FractionDigits constraints to the IR and XSD restriction parsing.

title: Documentation site
description: Create a documentation site covering: getting started (5-minute Gradle plugin setup), programmatic API guide with code examples, schema compatibility matrix (which XSD/JSON Schema features are supported), annotation modes guide, configuration reference, and contributing guide. Use MkDocs Material or Docusaurus. Host on GitHub Pages.

title: Publish to Maven Central
description: Set up publishing configuration to release schema2class artifacts to Maven Central (Sonatype OSSRH). Requires: POM metadata (description, URL, licenses, developers, SCM), GPG signing configuration, Nexus staging workflow. Publish: core, parser-xsd, parser-jsonschema, codegen-kotlin, cli (fat jar), gradle-plugin (to Gradle Plugin Portal). Document the release process.

title: Value class generation for constrained simple types
description: Optionally generate @JvmInline value classes instead of type aliases for XSD simpleType restrictions and JSON Schema string/number types with constraints (minLength, maxLength, minimum, maximum, pattern). Value classes provide compile-time type safety. Must document the tradeoff: value classes can't be nullable without boxing. Make this opt-in via config.
close_reason: Added opt-in @JvmInline value class generation for constrained AliasTypes and exposed it through CLI and Gradle config.

title: Annotation mode: Jackson
description: Emit Jackson annotations as a codegen mode. Must cover BOTH JSON and XML flavors: plain Jackson (@JsonProperty for schemaName≠kotlinName mappings) for JSON Schema-sourced models, and jackson-dataformat-xml (@JacksonXmlProperty isAttribute=..., @JacksonXmlElementWrapper, @JacksonXmlText for simpleContent contentProperty) for XSD-sourced models. Jackson XML is the JVM-only alternative to the xmlutil mode (schema2class-1k6) — see docs/domain-survey.md. Uses PropertyKind (schema2class-jo4) to pick attribute vs element annotations.
close_reason: Completed Jackson annotation mode and documented DTD/RELAX NG via trang conversion with CLI guidance.

title: Review direct DTD and RELAX NG parser support
description: Revisit the current trang-conversion-only decision for DTD and RELAX NG. Evaluate maintenance cost, user value, available libraries, and whether direct parser support or a bundled/wrapped conversion path is justified.

title: DTD and RELAX NG input via trang conversion (docs + optional CLI wrapping)
description: Per docs/domain-survey.md: do NOT write DTD or RELAX NG parsers. trang (James Clark, BSD, jing-trang project) converts DTD and RELAX NG (XML + compact syntax) to XSD and is stable though barely maintained. Deliverable: a documented recipe (DTD/RNG → trang → XSD → schema2class) validated against samples/xbel-1.0.dtd, and optionally a CLI convenience flag that shells out to or embeds trang. Covers the legacy-DTD and publishing (DocBook/TEI/ODF) niches at near-zero maintenance cost.
close_reason: Completed Jackson annotation mode and documented DTD/RELAX NG via trang conversion with CLI guidance.

title: Migrate from Kotlin 1.9.x to Kotlin 2.x (K2 compiler)
description: Once Kotlin 2.x is the clear ecosystem default, migrate the project from 1.9.x. Steps: upgrade kotlin() plugin version, resolve any K2 compiler warnings/errors (K2 is stricter about some type inference), update KotlinPoet and other deps to versions that support 2.x, run full test suite. Check that the Gradle plugin still works with the K2 compiler in the consuming project. Do not rush this — wait until major dependencies (KotlinPoet, kotlinx.serialization) have stable 2.x support.

value: Project owner has direct use cases for BOTH XSD and JSON Schema generation. Their format (clarified July 2026): an .xsd and a schema.json together define valid XML payloads — one XML element's text content is a JSON document conforming to the JSON Schema. This is NOT cross-format IR linkage: the JSON-bearing element stays String in the envelope class; the payload class is generated from the schema.json independently. What it requires is mixed-format input support in the Gradle plugin/CLI (schema2class-sqb). Sequencing: XSD work first, but JSON Schema features are NOT deprioritized — both halves are load-bearing.

KotlinPoet 1.18.1 backtick-escapes 'value' as a soft keyword: ComplexType.contentProperty named 'value' generates `value` in data class constructors. Valid Kotlin but tests must not assert on the unescaped form (assert on the type instead). If ergonomics matter later, let the XSD parser pick a domain name for the content property.
